### Post by Frank Golden on 2006-12-01
Hi parradux,

Try this guide
use second method in the guide. This link is for Dapper.

[http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide)

This one (below) is for Edgy

[http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide)
Again use the second method.

Follow the directions exactly. Copy and paste as much as possible.

Keep these instructions handy (bookmark em). You will need them every time you do a kernel update.

Good Luck. This guide has never failed me.
BTW, what card do you have.

### Post by apokalipto on 2006-12-01
Hi!!!

A couple of weeks ago I had to strugle in order to get the Beryl working with my ati card.

You should try using the "radeon" or "ati" drivers. those have 3D accel support for your card. They work great with mine.. which is a Mobility 7500.

### Post by sad_iq on 2006-12-02
> It fails on this command: sudo rm /usr/src/fglrx-kernel*.deb
 
 I get this message: rm: cannot remove `/usr/src/fglrx-kernel*.deb': No such file or directory

That is not an error...it just tells you there are no fglrx-kernel*.deb files in /usr/src/   ...you need this comand if you update the kernel after you installed the graphics driver so it removes stuff related with the old kernel.
 Just make sure you blacklist the fglrx module from linux-restricted-modules.Also...are you using the right guide?
It is not the same in Edgy as in Dapper, just make sure you use the right one!!!
 If it still fails...try to remove anything related to Beryl or Compiz so they do not mess with your install!!
 You must excuse my english(not native speaker)...and my keyboard is also broken :mad:

---
